title: Trump post appearing to depict him as Jesus removed amid backlash
description: Before the post was deleted, Evangelical and Catholic allies called the image blasphemous in a rare public break from a base that has largely stood by Trump.

title: Judge dismisses Trump’s lawsuit against Wall Street Journal over Epstein drawing
description: A federal judge on Monday dismissed a lawsuit filed by President Donald Trump that claimed that the Wall Street Journal defamed him by publishing a drawing of a naked woman it said Trump contributed to a birthday gift for Jeffrey Epstein.

title: White House says it’s boosting affordability through physician reforms
description: A new report distills the Trump administration’s strategy as officials jockey to shape health care messages ahead of this year’s midterm elections.

title: Lambasting Pope Leo, Trump risks alienating conservative Catholics
description: Veteran church observers say an open war of words between a pope and a U.S. president is unprecedented.

title: Pope responds to president’s insults: ‘I have no fear of the Trump administration’
description: The president wrote that the first U.S.-born pope, who has criticized the Iran war, was “weak” and “terrible” on crime and foreign policy.

title: Judge dismisses Trump's $10B lawsuit over the Wall Street Journal's Epstein reporting
description: In the order issued Monday, the judge wrote that President Trump had failed to make the argument that the article, which described a letter to Epstein that the newspaper said bore Trump's signature, was published with the intent to be malicious.

title: New disclosures reveal how DOGE actually worked
description: Depositions offer insight into what Elon Musk’s group was up to. Members describe a club-like atmosphere in which they slashed agencies with little oversight.

title: As Iran war drags on, midterm forecasts for Republicans get even worse
description: The GOP was already bracing for a tough election, but with the ceasefire in doubt, gas prices are likely to stay high, and voters’ economic outlook is dismal.

title: Under attack by their own congressman
description: Muslims in Rep. Chip Roy’s Texas district react to their Republican congressman’s campaign against Islam with concern — and eye-rolls.

title: How a $75 billion windfall from Congress has insulated ICE
description: Last year, Congress approved $75 billion for immigration enforcement. That money has allowed ICE to operate nearly unfettered during a record-long shutdown of the Department of Homeland Security.

title: 2025 was one of most volatile years ever for U.S. naturalizations
description: The year began with many people becoming U.S. citizens, but by December, fewer people were doing so, driven by ramped-up scrutiny of applications and eroding trust in the system.

title: Rep. Eric Swalwell suspends his bid for California governor amid assault allegations
description: U.S. Rep. Eric Swalwell is suspending his campaign for governor of California, as he faces allegations of sexual assault and misconduct.

title: Congress returns to Washington with lengthy to-do list
description: Congress returns this week after a two-week recess and they have a long to-do list.

title: New report suggests Democrats have better odds in some upcoming Senate races
description: NPR's A Martinez asks Jessica Taylor of the Cook Political Report about a new report on the midterm outlook for U.S. Senate races.

title: Voters grill Republican lawmaker in crucial swing district on Iran war
description: The conflict has animated voters in the suburban swing district of Republican Rep. Mike Lawler ahead of the midterm elections.

title: Swalwell suspends campaign for Calif. governor amid sexual misconduct claims
description: Several Democratic and Republican lawmakers said they would be in favor of voting to expel Swalwell, as well as Rep. Tony Gonzales (R-Texas), who both face misconduct allegations.

title: Pope Leo says he does not fear Trump, as he pushes back in feud over Iran war
description: President Donald Trump delivered an extraordinary broadside against Pope Leo XIV on Sunday night, saying he didn't think the U.S.-born global leader of the Catholic Church is "doing a very good job."

title: Trump announces naval blockade of Iran after Islamabad talks yield no deal
description: The blockade could derail a tenuous ceasefire after just five days. Vice President JD Vance said the U.S. remains open to diplomacy if Iran takes “our final and best offer.”

title: Hungary’s Viktor Orban, ally of Trump and Putin, concedes election defeat
description: WIth record turnout, Hungarians chose to end the 16-year rule of the prime minister who was a self-proclaimed champion of illiberal Christian democracy.

title: Fighting assault allegations, Eric Swalwell suspends his bid for California governor
description: The San Francisco Chronicle and CNN reported multiple allegations of sexual assault and misconduct. Swalwell calls them false but apologized to his supporters and family.

title: Inside Vance’s Iran negotiations: No deal, but ‘friendly’ talks
description: Vice President JD Vance’s negotiations in Pakistan with Iran failed to end the war, but progress was reported on building goodwill.

title: Hungary's Viktor Orbán concedes defeat, ending 16 years in power
description: Hungarian voters turned out in the greatest numbers since the 1990s to turn away from Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's right-wing populist Fidesz party.

title: Bust of multi-state drug ring highlights increasing use of police drones
description: Once a novelty, drones are now being flown by more than 5,000 large and small law enforcement agencies across the nation.

title: Heritage president toasted editor of controversial right-wing magazine
description: Heritage Foundation chief Kevin Roberts called Paul Gottfried, a “paleoconservative” who promotes writers accused of white supremacism, “one of the sages of our age.”

title: Democrats say GOP broke promise on prices, but some swing voters lost faith in change
description: President Donald Trump’s attack on Iran sent gas prices surging, giving Democrats’ focus on affordability more traction if they can get voters to trust them.

title: At a mahjong table in Iowa, a struggle over which party to trust
description: Women courted in key races weigh their choices — and whether the president’s policies have delivered for a working-class town hit by tariffs, war and rising costs.

title: Congress returns Monday with a long list: Iran war funding, airport security and more
description: Congress is back Monday with a big to-do list: DHS funding, renewal of section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act, and 1.5 trillion dollars for the Department of Defense.

title: Swalwell staffers stand with accusers as Manhattan DA confirms investigation
description: The California Democratic gubernatorial candidate is facing growing pressure to withdraw from the race after multiple women accused him of sexual misconduct.

title: Federal judges say White House ballroom construction can continue, for now
description: President Donald Trump has claimed that halting the project poses a risk to national security and threatens him.
